events.description: User clicked on the search input field to focus it.
events.frame_description: The search input field transitions from an inactive state to a focused state with a visible border.

events.description: User hovered over the help icon in the bottom left of the sidebar.
events.frame_description: The cursor is positioned over a small question mark icon at the bottom of the navigation sidebar.

events.description: User clicked the '+ Create' button in the sidebar, which opened the 'New study' selection menu.
events.frame_description: The cursor is positioned over a button in the sidebar; clicking it triggers a 'New study' menu to appear on the right.

events.description: User clicks on 'Interview study' in the 'New study' dropdown menu, which triggers a navigation to the study setup page.
events.frame_description: The user selects the 'Interview study' option from an open dropdown menu.

events.description: The browser URL updates, removing the 'research-type' query parameter as the page finishes loading or redirects.
events.frame_description: Close-up of the browser address bar showing the URL changing.

events.description: User updated the study title, causing the breadcrumb to change from 'Askable Product Team -D26 - 12 participants' to 'Tv'
events.frame_description: The breadcrumb navigation at the top of the page updates to reflect a new study title.

events.description: User clicks the 'Next' button on the 'Give your study a title' page to proceed.
events.frame_description: The cursor is positioned over the 'Next' button while the study title 'TV Streaming - Discovery' is entered.

events.description: The application navigates from the study title setup to the research method selection page.
User selects 'Video Call' as the moderated research method.
events.page_title: How will you be conducting your research?
events.frame_description: The cursor is over the 'Video Call' option, which is highlighted with a blue selection border.

events.description: The application navigates to the 'Device requirements' setup page.
events.frame_description: The screen transitions from the research method selection to the device requirements selection.

events.description: User clicks the 'Next' button on the 'Device requirements' page, which triggers a navigation to the tool selection step.
User scrolls down the 'Which tool will you use to conduct the remote research?' page to view more options.
events.page_title: Which tool will you use to conduct the remote research?

events.description: The page navigates from the tool selection step to the 'Quota' step.
The 'Discovery' page content loads, displaying 'Step 1: What is the name of your project?'
events.frame_description: The main content area updates from a loading or empty state to show the first step of the Discovery phase.

events.description: User clicks on the 'Quota' sidebar item to navigate to the participant quota settings
events.frame_description: The sidebar shows the 'Quota' item highlighted, indicating the user has selected this section of the study setup.

events.description: User clicked the 'Next' button on the Session duration page, which triggered a navigation to the participant selection screen.
events.frame_description: The cursor is positioned over the 'Next' button in frame 0, and frame 1 shows the page has successfully navigated to the next step.

events.description: User hovers over the 'Hand picking' selection card on the Participant selection page.
events.frame_description: The cursor is positioned over the 'Hand picking' option, which is highlighted with a blue border.

events.description: User clicked the 'Next' button on the Participant Selection page, triggering a navigation to the Audience sourcing page.
events.frame_description: The cursor is positioned over the 'Next' button in frame 0, and frame 1 shows the application has loaded the next step in the workflow.

events.description: The page navigated from the Participant Selection setup to the Audience Panel selection screen.
User clicked the 'Next' button to move from sourcing participants to participant location.
events.frame_description: The user is on the 'How would you like to source participants' screen and clicks 'Next' with 'Askable Panel' selected.

events.description: User changed the participant location selection from Australia to United States.
events.frame_description: The selected location in the input field changes from 'Australia' to 'United States', and the map view updates accordingly.

events.description: The page navigated from Participant location to Demographic filters.
events.frame_description: The screen transitions from the map-based location selection to the demographic filters configuration page.

events.description: User moves the cursor to hover over the 'Exclude people you've contacted' dropdown menu.
events.frame_description: The user is on the Demographic filters page and moves their mouse towards the exclusion criteria dropdown.

events.description: User selects 'Don't exclude anyone' from the 'Exclude people you've already spoken to' dropdown menu
events.frame_description: The selection in the exclusion dropdown changes from 'In the last 3 months' to 'Don't exclude anyone'.

events.description: User selects 'Don't exclude anyone' from the 'Exclude people' dropdown menu
events.frame_description: The user is on the demographic filters page where they have selected to not exclude anyone from the audience.

events.description: User enters the first answer option: "I don't use them at all"
events.frame_description: The first answer option appears in the 'Answers' section of the screener modal.

events.description: User pauses for several seconds with the cursor over the sidebar area, likely considering the next answer option
events.frame_description: The cursor remains stationary over the darkened background area outside the modal.

events.description: User enters the second answer option: "I use them a few times per week"
events.frame_description: A second answer option is added to the list in the screener modal.

events.description: User navigated from the 'Your timezone' section under 'Additional Info' to the 'Custom screener' section under 'Audience' using the sidebar menu.
events.frame_description: The screen transitions from the timezone settings page to the custom screener creation page within the study setup workflow.

events.description: User hovers over a question item in the sidebar list, indicated by the grey highlight on the bottom item.
events.frame_description: The sidebar shows a list of questions with checkmarks, and the user is hovering over the last item.

events.description: A dropdown menu is opened for a question, likely to configure question types or options.
events.frame_description: A dropdown menu is visible over the question text 'Which best describes...', showing options like 'Answer' and 'Option'.

events.description: User pauses while typing or reviewing the screener question text '...e of your favourite TV show?'
events.frame_description: The user is focused on the input field for a short answer screener question, with the text partially visible.

events.description: User continues typing the screener question: 'What is the name of your favourite TV show that you're wa'
events.frame_description: Close-up of a text input field with partially typed question.

events.description: User clicks on a checkmark icon, likely to save or confirm the screener question.
events.frame_description: Cursor is over a green checkmark icon in a sidebar or list.

events.description: User enters 'Netflix' into the first answer field of the multiple choice screener.
events.frame_description: The modal shows the 'Answers' section with 'Netflix' typed into the first eligible option field.

events.description: User pauses on the screener modal after entering the first answer option, likely reviewing the input.
events.frame_description: The view returns to the top of the screener modal showing the question text.

events.description: User drags the 'Short Answer' question block to reorder it within the custom screener list.
events.frame_description: The cursor changes to a move icon as the user interacts with the drag handle of a Short Answer question.

events.description: User clicked on the 'Additional Info' menu item in the sidebar, navigating to the timezone settings page.
events.frame_description: The user is on the custom screener page and clicks the 'Additional Info' section in the left-hand navigation menu.

events.description: User clicked the 'Next' button on the timezone page, navigating to the description page.
events.frame_description: After navigating to the timezone page, the user immediately clicks the 'Next' button to proceed to the next step.

events.description: User navigates from the 'Short description' step to the 'Additional Instructions' step within the booking setup.
events.frame_description: The screen transitions from the description input fields to a page titled 'Additional Instructions'.

events.description: User clicks the 'Next' button on the Contacts page.
events.frame_description: The cursor is positioned directly over the 'Next' button at the bottom of the Contacts list.

events.description: The page navigates from the Contacts section to the Legal & Privacy section.
events.frame_description: The UI changes from the contact list to the Legal & Privacy selection screen.

events.description: The user navigates from the Legal & Privacy section to the Calendar view for session times.
events.frame_description: The screen transitions from the legal agreement options to a calendar interface.

events.description: Available time slots appear on the right side of the calendar interface, likely triggered by the selection of a date.
events.frame_description: The calendar shows February 25th selected, and in the subsequent frame, specific time slots (9 AM, 10 AM, 11 AM) become visible.

events.description: User navigates from the 'Review & Submit' page to the 'Participants' page after submitting the study for review.
events.frame_description: The screen transitions from the study review confirmation to the participants dashboard, showing a banner that the study is in review.

events.description: The placeholder rows in the participant table disappear, indicating a change in the page's loading state.
events.frame_description: Close-up of the participant table headers and empty rows.

events.description: User clicks on the browser address bar, highlighting the URL. Simultaneously, the 'All participants' sidebar becomes visible on the page.
The page reloads or navigates, resulting in a blank screen and a change in the tab title to 'Askable'.
A yellow banner appears at the top of the page stating that the study is currently in review.
